Complex Functions

Let @@math:25:50cca6@@ be a set of complex numbers. A [[em]]function[[/em]] @@math:26:b9c2ce@@ defined on @@math:27:73b73f@@ is a rule that assigns to
                    each @@math:28:b61f22@@ in @@math:29:b2b8be@@ a complex number @@math:30:8edad9@@ The number @@math:31:1e389a@@ is called the value of @@math:32:4df8d5@@ at @@math:33:a6928f@@ and is
                    denoted by @@math:34:d0fc98@@; that is, @@math:35:018425@@ The set @@math:36:1df7da@@ is called the domain of definition of @@math:37:e92613@@

                    a [[em]]single-valued[[/em]] function of @@math:41:d2f469@@ or that @@math:42:3a627f@@ is single-valued.
                    If more than one value of @@math:43:bc3e54@@ corresponds to each value of @@math:44:0480aa@@ we say
                    that @@math:45:27f0d3@@ is a [[em]]multiple-valued[[/em]] or [[em]]many-valued[[/em]] function of @@math:46:65a957@@

A [[em]]multiple-valued[[/em]] function can be considered as a collection of

                    of the function. In general, we consider one particular member as a
                    [[em]]principal branch[[/em]] of the multiple-valued function and the value
                    of the function corresponding to this branch as the [[em]]principal value[[/em]].
